//1: snowflake-claims-platform :: Claude Desktop MCP client configuration
//2: Copy the relevant block into your Claude Desktop config:
//5: All data in this platform is SYNTHETIC. There is no real PHI/PII.
//PRIMARY: PRIMARY (recommended): the SNOWFLAKE-MANAGED MCP server. This is a
//PRIMARY2: remote HTTP/SSE endpoint hosted by Snowflake that exposes Cortex Analyst,
//PRIMARY3: Cortex Search, Cortex Agents, and approved read-only SQL. Replace the
//PRIMARY4: <PLACEHOLDERS> below. Authenticate as the least-privilege CLAIMS_MCP_READER
//PRIMARY5: role using a short-lived Programmatic Access Token (PAT) or OAuth token.

snowflake-claims-managed.//note: PRIMARY. Snowflake-managed MCP (remote). Uses an HTTP/SSE bridge.
snowflake-claims-managed.//transport: Claude Desktop launches stdio servers; we bridge to the remote
snowflake-claims-managed.//transport2: HTTP/SSE Snowflake MCP endpoint via the mcp-remote helper.

env.//SNOWFLAKE_PAT: Short-lived Programmatic Access Token for a user whose
env.//SNOWFLAKE_PAT2: DEFAULT_ROLE is CLAIMS_MCP_READER. Do NOT hardcode in shared configs.

mcpServers.//FALLBACK1: FALLBACK ONLY (not primary): the local custom stdio MCP server
mcpServers.//FALLBACK2: under mcp/fallback_custom_server. Use ONLY when the Snowflake-managed
mcpServers.//FALLBACK3: MCP server is unavailable in your account. It is read-only, SELECT-only,
mcpServers.//FALLBACK4: schema-allowlisted, and logs to AUDIT.MCP_QUERY_LOG. To enable, REMOVE
mcpServers.//FALLBACK5: the leading underscore from the key below and fill the env values.

_snowflake-claims-fallback.//note: FALLBACK ONLY. Local Python stdio server. Prefer the managed server above.

env.//auth: Key-pair auth preferred over password. Set ONE of the two below.

mcpServers.//FALLBACK_SSO1: FALLBACK ONLY (local-dev SSO variant): same local stdio server,
mcpServers.//FALLBACK_SSO2: but authenticating with externalbrowser (Snowflake SSO). This opens a
mcpServers.//FALLBACK_SSO3: browser window for the developer to log in. Only works on a machine
mcpServers.//FALLBACK_SSO4: WITH a browser (not headless). For headless use the key-pair block above.
mcpServers.//FALLBACK_SSO5: Enable by removing the leading underscore from the key below.

_snowflake-claims-fallback-sso.//note: FALLBACK ONLY. Local Python stdio server with externalbrowser (SSO) auth.

env.//auth: externalbrowser = Snowflake SSO. No key/password needed locally.
